sub_pixel_system.notes: Not documented in available source material

special_mechanics.name: Block Cursor Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player controls an on-screen cursor to arrange and swap colored blocks. Blocks can be arranged into horizontal or vertical rows.

special_mechanics.name: Block Falling/Gravity
special_mechanics.description: Blocks above cleared lines fall downward, enabling chain reactions when they contact matching blocks below.

special_mechanics.name: Rising Stack
special_mechanics.description: Blocks steadily rise towards the top of the playfield, with new blocks generating from the bottom. Speed increases as stage progresses. Game over occurs when blocks reach the top.

physics_values.difficulty_escalation: blocks rise and speed increases over time
physics_values.win_condition: blocks must not reach top of playfield

feel_descriptors: slower paced compared to action games
tier_notes: Tier 2: Panel de Pon is a puzzle game without traditional character physics. The available Wikipedia source contains gameplay mechanics but no precise movement, gravity, or jump physics parameters. All physics-related fields are null as the game does not involve platforming or character movement with measurable velocity/acceleration values.
